# LLM Gateway

> Lightweight LLM API gateway with multi-provider routing, automatic fallback, and sticky routing.

## Authentication

### Management API (/api/*)
Requires admin key when ADMIN_KEY env var is set.
Pass via: `x-admin-key` header, `Authorization: Bearer <key>`, or `?admin_key=<key>` query param.

### Proxy Endpoints (/v1/*)
Requires a gateway API key (prefix `gw-`) when any API keys exist.
Pass via: `Authorization: Bearer gw-xxx` or `x-api-key: gw-xxx`.
If no API keys exist, proxy is open (setup mode).

### Health Check
GET /health — no auth, always accessible.

## API (Management)

Base: http://localhost:3456/api

### Providers
- GET /api/providers — list all providers (apiKeys are masked in response)
- POST /api/providers — create provider { name, baseUrl, apiKey, apiType: "openai"|"openai-responses"|"anthropic"|"gemini" }
- PUT /api/providers/:id — update provider
- DELETE /api/providers/:id — delete provider
- POST /api/providers/:id/test — test provider connection
- POST /api/providers/:id/fetch-models — auto-discover models from provider

### Models
- GET /api/models — list all models (includes deployments)
- POST /api/models — create model { name }
- PUT /api/models/:id — update model { name }
- DELETE /api/models/:id — delete model (cascades to deployments, cleans chains)

### Deployments (model → provider binding)
- GET /api/deployments — list all deployments
- POST /api/deployments — create { modelId, providerId, modelName, order, timeout, maxRetries }
- PUT /api/deployments/:id — update { order, timeout, maxRetries, enabled (0|1), modelName }
- DELETE /api/deployments/:id — delete deployment

### Chains (fallback sequences)
- GET /api/chains — list chains
- POST /api/chains — create { name, mode: "models"|"provider", items: JSON string }
- PUT /api/chains/:id — update chain { name, mode, items, enabled }
- DELETE /api/chains/:id — delete chain

Chain modes:
- "models": items = ["model-name-1", "model-name-2"] — tries each model in order
- "provider": items = [{"provider": "name", "models": ["m1","m2"]}, ...] — tries specific models from specific providers

### Sticky/Pin
- POST /api/sticky — pin deployment { modelName, deploymentId, ttlMs? }
- DELETE /api/sticky/:model — clear sticky for model/chain (URL-encode model name)
- DELETE /api/sticky — clear all sticky routes

Sticky behavior:
- Stored in memory (lost on restart)
- Default TTL: 2 hours
- Manual pin (via POST) takes priority over auto-sticky
- Chain-level sticky uses chain name as key

### API Keys
- GET /api/keys — list all keys (full key visible for copy)
- POST /api/keys — create { name, rateLimit?, allowedModels?: "model1,model2" }
- PUT /api/keys/:id — update { name, enabled, rateLimit, allowedModels }
- DELETE /api/keys/:id — delete key

### Stats & Logs
- GET /api/stats — summary, deployment stats, cooldowns, sticky info
- GET /api/logs?limit=N&offset=N&model=X&status=N&provider=X — request logs
- GET /api/model-stats — per-model aggregated stats
- GET /api/model-timeline?hours=24 — hourly request counts per model

### Route Testing
- POST /api/test-route — test routing { model, message?, providerId? }
  - Without providerId: tests full routing chain (skips sticky)
  - With providerId: direct provider test (bypasses routing)

## Proxy (LLM requests)
- POST /v1/chat/completions — OpenAI-compatible (requires gw- API key)
- POST /v1/responses — OpenAI Responses API compatible (requires gw- API key)
- GET /v1/responses/:response_id — retrieve stored response metadata target and proxy upstream response
- POST /v1/embeddings — embeddings proxy (requires gw- API key)
- POST /v1/rerank — rerank proxy (requires gw- API key)
- POST /v1/messages — Anthropic-compatible (requires gw- API key)
- GET /v1/models — list models (no auth)

Model name in request body maps to a model or chain name in the gateway.
If the model name matches a chain, the gateway routes through the chain's fallback sequence.

## Routing Behavior

1. Check if model name matches a fallback chain
2. If chain: try sticky deployment first, then chain items in order
3. If no chain: try deployments for that model in priority order
4. Each deployment: retry with exponential backoff (2s, 4s, 8s, 16s cap)
5. After 3 consecutive failures: deployment enters cooldown (2+ minutes)
6. On success: set auto-sticky for 2 hours, record EMA latency

## Typical Setup Flow

1. Create a provider (name, baseUrl, apiKey, apiType)
2. Fetch or manually create models
3. Create deployments linking models to providers
4. Create API key for your application
5. Optionally create a chain for fallback routing
6. Point your app at the gateway's /v1/chat/completions, /v1/responses, /v1/embeddings, /v1/rerank, or /v1/messages with `Authorization: Bearer gw-xxx`
